mnemonics: RSTX - Receive, Send, Transmit, X - eXceed Buffer
keyPoints: The Sliding Window Protocol is used to prevent network congestion and ensure reliable data transfer.
The window size is set by the sender and is typically fixed or adaptive.
When the sender's buffer is full, it sends an ACK to the receiver and then waits for a chunk of data before sending the next packet.
If the receiver receives a packet before the sender is ready, it sends a NACK to the sender, who then retransmits the missing data.
The Sliding Window Protocol allows for efficient data transfer and error recovery.
